t_ch: 你还真不客气啊。
t_en: Huh, couldn't even give it a minute, could you?

t_ch: 鼹鼠党的原则是互帮互助！…拜托你了嘛。
t_en: Members of The Moles gotta help each other out! ...Please...

t_ch: 只要虎克不惹事的话…
t_en: As long as you behave yourself...

t_ch: 我才不会帮你。
t_en: I don't want to help you.

t_ch: …然后你会一直问我。
t_en: ...But you'll just keep pestering me.

t_ch: …然后我会良心不安。
t_en: ...And then I'll feel bad about not helping you.
I—I mean... Argh...

t_ch: 怎么了，年轻人？还有什么事吗？
t_en: What's wrong, young one? Is something else on your mind?

t_ch: 矿脉探测器是个什么玩意？
t_en: What is a vein detector?

t_ch: 是我以前在矿坑里捡到的古代遗物，是专门用来探测地髓的非金属矿石，非常稀有，我从未见过有第二个。
t_en: It's an ancient relic that I found in a mine. People say it's a device that mining teams used for detecting Geomarrow veins long ago. It's very rare — I haven't seen a second one in my life.

t_ch: 费斯曼大叔收养了虎克？
t_en: You adopted Hook?

t_ch: 没错。我和虎克来自北方的一个工业小镇，但那里已经被裂界侵蚀了。我们不得已才成了流浪者。
t_en: Yes. Hook and I were from a small industrial town up north, but that place became corroded by the Fragmentum, forcing us to leave, as vagrants.

t_ch: 虎克的父母去世之前跟我关系很好，算是忘年交。娜塔莎问我能不能收养虎克的时候，就毫不犹豫地答应了。
t_en: Hook's parents were close friends of mine, though I'm much older than them. When Natasha asked me if I could adopt Hook, I agreed without hesitation.

t_ch: 在裂界彻底侵蚀孤儿院之后，娜塔莎帮每个孩子都找好了收养家庭。那姑娘，真是厉害啊……
t_en: When the Fragmentum completely overtook the Orphanage, Natasha found adoptive families for every single child. She sure is one incredible lady...

t_ch: 什么时候丢的，有印象吗？
t_en: Do you have any idea when you lost your treasure?

t_ch: 你们在外面解决矿区冲突的时候，怕事的流浪者们躲在避难所里。直到那个时候，我的探测器都还在身上。
t_en: When you were out resolving that big conflict in the mine, some of the more cautious vagrants like myself hid in the shelter. I still had my detector at that point.

t_ch: 冲突结束之后，「地火」需要彻底清点伤员，我们就暂时离开了避难所。清点完人数以后再回去，我就发现探测器不见了。
t_en: When the fighting was over, Wildfire asked everyone to leave the shelter to get a headcount of the wounded. After that, I went back to the shelter, only to find that my detector was gone.

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: I don't have anything else to ask about.

t_ch: 我不在时候，就麻烦你们照顾虎克了。
t_en: When I'm gone, please help look after Hook.

t_ch: 太慢了！鼹鼠党荣誉队员{NICKNAME}，你应该比虎克先到才对！
t_en: Too slow, {NICKNAME}, Honorary Member of The Moles! You should have gotten here before Hook!

t_ch: 给你介绍一下，这位是老爹最信任的矿工，斯薇塔姐姐！
t_en: Let me introduce you to Swetta. She is daddy's most trusted miner.

t_ch: 不是这样的，费斯曼大叔对流浪者们都很信任，大家也都信任着大叔。
t_en: Not at all, Mr. Fersman trusts every vagrant, and every one of us trusts Mr. Fersman.

t_ch: 但是经历了矿脉事件之后…避难所的气氛就变得微妙了起来。
t_en: But after what happened over at that Geomarrow vein... the atmosphere at the shelter became a bit tense.

t_ch: 虎克过来是找老爹的「矿脉探测器」的。听说有人把老爹的宝贝给偷走了，是真的吗？
t_en: I'm here to find daddy's treasure, his vein detector! I heard that someone stole it, is that true?

t_ch: 这…让我猜测的话，大概是真的。
t_en: Well... I would guess that's probably true.

t_ch: 矿山冲突结束之后，「地火」把避难所的人都集合了起来。为了确定伤员情况，还清点过一次人数，<color=#dbc291ff>当时有四个人不在场</color>。
t_en: After the conflict in the mines, Wildfire gathered everyone at the shelter and took a headcount to check on the wounded. <color=#dbc291ff>Four people were not there.</color>

t_ch: 我们回来以后，费斯曼大叔的探测器就不见了。我们翻遍了整个避难所也没有找到…我猜，<color=#dbc291ff>小偷应该就在这四人之中</color>。
t_en: When we returned to the shelter, Mr. Fersman's detector was gone. We searched all around the shelter but found nothing... I guess <color=#dbc291ff>one of those four people was the thief</color>.

t_ch: 那个时候避难所里没有别人。
t_en: No one else was at the shelter during that time.

t_ch: 没错，避难所平时一直很熙攘，小偷很难找到机会下手。
t_en: Yes. The shelter was usually crowded, so thieves would've had a hard time stealing things when everyone was around.

t_ch: 犯人挑错了时间。
t_en: They picked the wrong time to commit the crime.

t_ch: 没错，嫌疑人的范围一下就缩小了。
t_en: Indeed, now we can narrow down the scope of our search considerably.

t_ch: 流浪者也有流浪者的尊严，我们一定会把小偷给抓出来。不过……
t_en: We vagrants have our dignity, too. I'm sure we will catch the thief, but...

t_ch: 不过什么啊？
t_en: But... what?

t_ch: 其实，我之前已经委婉地问过那几个嫌疑人了…但他们都声称自己没偷过东西。
t_en: Actually, we've already asked those four suspects about it... but they all claimed to not have stolen anything.

t_ch: 当然啦，也不能指望真正的小偷能老老实实认罪，是吧？依我看，<color=#dbc291ff>这四个人中肯定有一个在说谎</color>。
t_en: Of course, we could never expect thieves to confess and turn themselves in, right? In my opinion, <color=#dbc291ff>one of those four was lying</color>.

t_ch: 说谎的人就是真正的小偷。
t_en: The liar must be the thief.

t_ch: 没错——大家都不想惹麻烦，没犯错何必说假话呢？
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: 就不能超过一个吗？
t_en: There can't be more than one?

t_ch: 我想…应该不会。问心无愧的人何必说谎呢？
t_en: Hmm, doesn't seem likely.

t_ch: 这四个嫌疑人都在避难所里。你们如果有自信的话，不妨再去问问看——说不定这回能找到真正的犯人。
t_en: All four suspects are at the shelter. If you're feeling confident, why not go give it a try? Maybe you can determine who the thief is.

t_ch: 唔，大人可真是狡猾！虎克要一个一个问问看！
t_en: Ugh, adults are so dishonest! Hook's gonna question them one by one!

t_ch: 全部抓起来！逐个排查！
t_en: Arrest everyone and interrogate them one by one!

t_ch: 我们这里谁都没有这样的权利随随便便将谁抓起来，大家都是流浪者。
t_en: We're all vagrants. None of us have the authority to arrest anyone just because we feel like it.

t_ch: 但是没有嫌疑的人应该不会说谎，这就足以帮助我们抓住小偷了。
t_en: Let's see whose words contradict with the facts, and that person would be the thief.

t_ch: 哎哟哟，瞧瞧这二位，在这儿演侦探呢？
t_en: Well, well, well! What's this, playing detective, are we?

t_ch: 说，是不是你把老爹的探测器偷走了？
t_en: Spill it! Did you steal my daddy's detector?

t_ch: 这小鬼！上来就给我扣一顶小偷的帽子，一看就是欠管教！我这人光明磊落，从来不干那小偷小摸的事。
t_en: You little brat! How dare you label me a thief! Someone needs to give you a good lesson on manners! I'm an honest and decent man, I'd never do such a shady thing!

t_ch: 哼，嘴上说说谁不会啊！你要是真的没偷东西，就乖乖回答我们的提问！
t_en: Hmph, anyone can say that... If you really didn't steal anything, then just answer our questions!

t_ch: 唷，小鬼脾气还挺冲？行，我就花两分钟听听你们想问什么。
t_en: Oh, you have a temper, huh? Alright, I'll spare a couple minutes for your little detective game.

t_ch: 案发当时你在干嘛？
t_en: What were you doing when the crime occurred?

t_ch: 做饭啊，还能干嘛？这一身油烟味还带骗人的吗？
t_en: I'm a cook, what else would I be doing? Don't you notice the smoky cooking smell all over me?

t_ch: 只能说时间不巧，我正好在那个时候去采购食材了——要不然谁想卷进这破事里来？
t_en: It's just that I happened to be out shopping for ingredients at that time. Otherwise, who'd want anything to do with this kind of thing?

t_ch: 你怎么看待费斯曼大叔？
t_en: What do you think of Mr. Fersman?

t_ch: 费斯曼老爷子对我还不赖，以前在铆钉镇的时候，他把整个矿队的伙食都包给我了。
t_en: That old Fersman was kind to me. When we were at Rivet Town, he put me in charge of the whole mining team's food supply.

t_ch: 也是因为信得过他，我才跟着他跑来这片矿区。没成想他居然会独吞那笔横财……
t_en: And it's because I trusted him that I came to this mine with him. I mean, I didn't expect he would take that money for himself...

t_ch: 嗐，毕竟矿脉是人家发现的，我也怪不得他。只能说，这么干不符合我一直以来对他的印象。
t_en: Anyway, he found that Geomarrow vein by himself, so I won't blame him for not sharing. But y'know, what he did was not like the old Fersman I knew.

t_ch: 你看到探测器了吗？
t_en: Did you see the detector?

t_ch: 嗯…不是我想当告密的小人啊，我把这秘密抖给你们，纯粹是看在费斯曼老爷子的面子上。
t_en: Well... I don't want to be a snitch, but out of my respect for Fersman, I'll tell you a secret.

t_ch: 那边的保姆，我之前看见他偷偷进了费斯曼老爷子的帐篷，然后带着个箱子出来了。你们说，他是不是嫌疑最大？
t_en: The helper over there, I saw him sneak into Fersman's tent and come out holding a box. See? Isn't he the most suspicious one?

t_ch: 嫌疑大不大，你说了不算。
t_en: Who is the most suspicious one is not up to you.

t_ch: 也可能是你在栽赃。
t_en: You might be framing him.

t_ch: 哼，爱信不信，反正我知道的都已经告诉你们了。问完了就快走吧，我这还忙着呢。
t_en: Hehe, I don't care if you believe me or not. Told you all I know. Now leave me alone if you don't have any more questions, I'm busy.

t_ch: 都跟你说了，肯定就是那个保姆干的。
t_en: I told you. I'm sure that helper is the thief.

t_ch: 我相信你，但是我说了不算。
t_en: I believe you, but it's not up to me to judge.

t_ch: 是你把老爹的探测器偷走了吗？
t_en: Did you steal daddy's detector?

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: Nope.

t_ch: 我才不是小偷。
t_en: I'm no thief.

t_ch: 真的吗？那你要老实回答我们几个问题！
t_en: Really? Then you better answer our questions honestly!

t_ch: 问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 别耽误时间。
t_en: Let's not waste time.

t_ch: 案发当时你在干嘛？
t_en: What were you doing when the crime occurred?

t_ch: 发呆。
t_en: Spacing out.

t_ch: 没有注意别的事。
t_en: Didn't notice anything.

t_ch: 你怎么看待费斯曼大叔？
t_en: What do you think of Mr. Fersman?

t_ch: 好人。
t_en: Good man.

t_ch: 不过爱贪小便宜。
t_en: But a bit of a cheapskate.

t_ch: 你看到探测器了吗？
t_en: Did you see the detector?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 什么探测器？
t_en: What detector?

t_ch: 你这家伙，就不能好好说话嘛！
t_en: Hey, would you mind talking more nicely?

t_ch: 怎么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 我平时就这么说话。
t_en: I always talk like this.

t_ch: …虎克感觉，这个姐姐不是小偷。
t_en: ...I don't feel like she is the thief.

t_ch: 你的感觉准吗？
t_en: Can we trust your hunch?

t_ch: …反正就是，老大才有的直觉！
t_en: ...Well, it's the hunch of Pitch Dark Hook the Great...

t_ch: 现在还不能下断言。
t_en: We shouldn't jump to conclusions yet.

t_ch: …虎克知道！
t_en: ...I know!

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Keep it moving.

t_ch: 我没偷什么探测器。
t_en: I didn't steal any detector.

t_ch: 是你把老爹的探测器偷走的嘛？
t_en: Did you steal daddy's detector?

t_ch: 什么，你凭什么诬赖我？叔叔我啊，要生气了！
t_en: What? How could you say that! You're gonna make me angry!

t_ch: 我觉得你很可疑。
t_en: I think you're suspicious.

t_ch: 不要紧张，这只是正常的询问。
t_en: Don't get upset. It's a perfectly reasonable question.

t_ch: 你知道商人最重要的是什么吗？信誉！你们这样凭空污蔑，万一影响到了我的生意…那可是要负责任的！
t_en: You know what a businessman values the most? Reputation! If your slander ruins my reputation and harms my business... You'll pay for that!

t_ch: 唔…那，那你好好回答我们的问题，我再决定该不该信你！
t_en: Uh... Well, then answer our questions! We'll decide if we believe you or not.

t_ch: 案发当时你在干嘛？
t_en: What were you doing when the crime occurred?

t_ch: 我有权保持沉默。
t_en: I have the right to remain silent.

t_ch: 你怎么看待费斯曼大叔？
t_en: What do you think of Mr. Fersman?

t_ch: 这个问题…我选择不发表意见。
t_en: I... withhold my opinion toward this question.

t_ch: 你看到探测器了吗？
t_en: Did you see the detector?

t_ch: 我拒绝评论。
t_en: No comment.

t_ch: 唔…你这家伙！
t_en: Argh... You!

t_ch: 虎克再给你一次机会，你看着洞洞机再好好想想！
t_en: I'll give you one more chance. Think carefully and give your answer to Diggertron this time!

t_ch: 虎克冷静，别吓着人家。
t_en: Calm down, Hook. Don't scare him.

t_ch: 区区小鬼还敢威胁叔叔我？你还早了几十年呢！
t_en: You little brat. Grow a couple feet taller before you think about threatening me!

t_ch: 看着我的拳头，再好好想想。
t_en: Think carefully and give your answer to my fists.

t_ch: 叔叔我什么大风大浪没见过，怎么会被你们这对大小流氓吓到？
t_en: I've seen all sorts of things in my life. Why would I be afraid of you two little rascals?

t_ch: 这样，来笔交易如何？只要一点点情报费，我就把自己掌握的信息告诉你们，保证童叟无欺！
t_en: ...Here, how about we make a deal? I'll tell you what I know, in exchange for a small intel service fee — fair and honest!

t_ch: 成交。
t_en: Deal.

t_ch: 爽快！
t_en: Great!

t_ch: 叔叔我啊，就喜欢不还价的顾客，这叫格局！来，想问什么，尽管开口！
t_en: I like your style! Customers who don't fuss over the price are the best. So what do you wanna know? Ask away!

t_ch: 想都别想。
t_en: That's not gonna happen.

t_ch: 没辙，那我只能继续行使沉默权了。你们看着办吧！
t_en: Well, looks like I can only continue exercising my right to remain silent. It's up to you.

t_ch: 行吧，我买你的情报。
t_en: Alright, I'll pay for your intel.

t_ch: 聪明人办聪明事，叔叔我啊，就喜欢跟聪明人做生意！
t_en: Smart decision. I love doing business with smart people!

t_ch: 快说！不然我喊「地火」来了。
t_en: Spill it or I'll call Wildfire over!

t_ch: 还想用「地火」来压我？说得好像我怕他们一样！
t_en: Trying to threaten me with Wildfire, huh? Think I'm afraid of them!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们把希儿姐姐叫过来，把他抓走！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's tell Seele to take him away!

t_ch: 啊？别别别，我就开个玩笑——行了，你们问吧，我说就是了！行行好，别叫「地火」过来！
t_en: Huh? No, please don't! I was only joking... Fine, I'll answer your questions! Please, just don't pull Wildfire into this.

t_ch: 案发当时你在干嘛？
t_en: What were you doing when the crime occurred?

t_ch: 去镇上进货了，有什么奇怪的吗？我的进货时间一直都很规律，随便谁都能替我作证。当时我还在好奇，避难所的人都跑哪去了？
t_en: I went into town to restock. Nothing out of the ordinary, right? I restock on a regular basis, everyone knows that. I was wondering why there was nobody at the shelter.

t_ch: 你怎么看待费斯曼大叔？
t_en: What do you think of Mr. Fersman?

t_ch: 大叔经常照顾我的生意，对我很和善，不过我跟他也说不上太熟。只说矿脉这事，他干得确实不地道。
t_en: Fersman is an old customer of mine. He's pretty nice to me, though we are not so close. As to what he did about that Geomarrow vein, it was kinda unethical.

t_ch: 肥水不流外人田，睁眼闭眼大家都是邻居，他这么胳膊肘往外拐…不合适，真不合适！
t_en: We're all neighbors. We've known each other for a long time. But he sold out to the other side and left his own people out to dry... I really can't agree with that!

t_ch: 你看到探测器了吗？
t_en: Did you see the detector?

t_ch: 绝对是那个厨子偷走的，千真万确。叔叔我啊，最注重信誉了，不会骗你们的。
t_en: Look, the cook is definitely the thief, I'm sure about that! I value my reputation more than anything, so you can trust me.

t_ch: 你怎么这么肯定？
t_en: Why are you so sure about that?

t_ch: 干我这行就得学会察言观色，那厨子心术不正，我老早就看出来了——不信你问别人。
t_en: In my line of work, I've learned to read people. And something's definitely fishy with that cook. I could tell from the start. If you don't believe me, just ask anyone.

t_ch: 感觉你还在骗我。
t_en: Feels like you're trying to trick me.

t_ch: 骗你我能捞着什么好处，等着「地火」半夜来敲我门么？
t_en: What do I have to gain from lying to you? Wildfire coming for me in the night?

t_ch: 信我吧，那厨子心术不正，我老早就看出来了——不信你问别人。
t_en: Trust me, something's definitely fishy with that cook. I could tell from the start. If you don't believe me, just ask anyone.

t_ch: 那厨子心术不正，费斯曼大叔的玩意绝对是他偷走的！
t_en: That cook is a crook. He's definitely the one who stole Fersman's device.

t_ch: 咦，虎克？怎么了，找我有事吗？
t_en: Huh, Hook? What's the matter?

t_ch: 是你把老爹的探测器偷走了吗？
t_en: Did you steal daddy's detector?

t_ch: 唉，早知道会被怀疑，我就不在那个时候出门了…我真傻。
t_en: Wha— Oh if I'd known I would become a suspect, I wouldn't have gone out at that time... How foolish of me.

t_ch: 别紧张，这是正常流程。
t_en: Relax. We're just checking off all the boxes.

t_ch: 心虚了？我看你就有问题。
t_en: Feeling guilty or something? You seem a bit off.

t_ch: 怎、怎么会呢！我又没干什么坏事……
t_en: O—Of course not! I didn't do anything wrong...

t_ch: 你只要回答我们几个问题就好！
t_en: We just need you to answer a few questions!

t_ch: 好…你们问吧。要是能帮我洗清嫌疑，那就太好了。
t_en: Okay... ask away. It would be good to clear myself of suspicion.

t_ch: 案发当时你在干嘛？
t_en: What were you doing when the crime occurred?

t_ch: 也没干什么特别的事，只是出去透了透气。
t_en: Nothing special. I just went out to get some fresh air.

t_ch: …完了完了，这个理由听着也太糟了！你不会因为这个就怀疑我吧？
t_en: Oh dear, oh dear... I know that's a lame alibi! But I swear it's the truth.

t_ch: 你怎么看待费斯曼大叔？
t_en: What do you think of Mr. Fersman?

t_ch: 费斯曼老爷子吗？他是个好人，虽然…呃，没什么。
t_en: Mr. Fersman? He's a good person. Though... oh, er, uh, never mind.

t_ch: …完了完了，我听上去是不是特别可疑？可我真的什么都没干，你们要相信我……
t_en: Oh no, oh no... I must sound very suspicious. But I really didn't do anything. You have to believe me...

t_ch: 你看到探测器了吗？
t_en: Did you see the detector?

t_ch: 探测器？没有，我什么都没看到，我什么都不知道……
t_en: Detector? No, I haven't seen anything, and I don't know anything...

t_ch: 你们问完了吗？能证明我是清白的了吗？
t_en: Is that all the questions you have? Have I proven my innocence?

t_ch: 先冷静点，等消息吧。
t_en: Just stay calm and wait for the news.

t_ch: 我心里有数了，你小心点儿。
t_en: I've figured it out. Be careful.

t_ch: 唉…就这么干等着，只会越来越焦虑啊……
t_en: Oh dear... All this waiting around is enough to make me even more anxious...

t_ch: 不是我偷的，真的不是我……
t_en: I didn't steal it. Really, it wasn't me...

t_ch: 啊…怎么办！虎克已经要晕了！你们还记得他们都说了什么吗？
t_en: Argh... Hook's head is spinning! Do you remember everything they said?

t_ch: 放心，我已经帮你们记下来了。记住，在这四个人里，应该<color=#dbc291ff>只有真正的小偷会说谎</color>。
t_en: Don't worry, I've written it down for you. Remember, among these four people, only the <color=#dbc291ff>real thief will lie</color>.

t_ch: 厨子说是保姆干的，裁缝说不是她干的，小贩说是厨子干的，保姆说不是他干的。怎么样，你知道谁是犯人了吗？
t_en: *sigh* The cook said the helper did it, the tailor said it wasn't her, the merchant said it was the cook, and the helper said it wasn't him. So, do you know who the culprit is?

t_ch: 没错，除了推理，我们还有别的办法能够找到真正的小偷。眼前就让我们先分析他们到底说了什么吧。
t_en: Right, we have other methods besides deductive reasoning to find out who the true thief is. Let's just examine what they've said first to decide.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>厨子。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The cook.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>裁缝。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The tailor.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>小贩。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The merchant.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>保姆。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The helper.</color>

t_ch: 虎克大人是什么建议呢？
t_en: Boss Hook, what do you think?

t_ch: 呣…虽然虎克也不知道为什么，但就暂且相信你的判断吧！
t_en: Hmm... I don't know why, but I'll trust your judgment!

t_ch: 走吧，我们去当面问问那个人！
t_en: Come on, let's go accuse the culprit!

t_ch: 我觉得最可疑还是<color=#dbc291ff>厨子</color>还有<color=#dbc291ff>小贩</color>。虎克的直觉一向很准，真正的犯人肯定是他们中的一个。
t_en: I think the <color=#dbc291ff>cook</color> and <color=#dbc291ff>merchant</color> are the most suspicious. My gut tells me that one of them is the culprit.

t_ch: 你要是选错了也没有关系，万能的虎克大人还会有一百种方法找到真正的犯人。
t_en: Don't worry. Even if you choose the wrong one, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great has other ways to uncover the true culprit.

t_ch: 咦！厨子呢，那个厨子跑哪里去了？
t_en: Huh!? Where's the cook? Where did he go?

t_ch: 我看他慌慌张张地跑到外面去了。
t_en: Beats me! I saw him running out in quite a panic.

t_ch: 啊，那家伙肯定是心虚了！会跑到哪里去呢……
t_en: Argh! That guy has to be guilty! Where is he running off to...

t_ch: 大概是急着去销赃吧。
t_en: Probably trying to sell off the spoils.

t_ch: 这不就是畏罪潜逃吗？
t_en: So he's fleeing from justice, huh?

t_ch: 啊，对了！在来矿区之前，虎克还在路上看到了桑博叔叔…这件事会不会跟他有关系啊？
t_en: Oh yeah! I saw Mr. Sampo on the way to the mine when I came here... Could he be involved in this?

t_ch: 绝对和他有关系！
t_en: It definitely has something to do with him!

t_ch: 他就是个行走的麻烦…
t_en: He's just trouble on legs.

t_ch: 虎克来带路，桑博叔叔应该就在那个方向！
t_en: Follow Hook, Mr. Sampo should be that way!

t_ch: 桑博应该是清白的！
t_en: Surely Sampo is innocent!

t_ch: 要是这么想就太天真了。
t_en: ...Heh, that would be a naive thing to believe.

t_ch: 虎克知道了，偷走老爹探测器的犯人就是你！
t_en: Hook figured it out, you're the one who stole daddy's detector!

t_ch: 不对。
t_en: Wrong.

t_ch: 不对？怎么不对，你倒是说说看啊！
t_en: Wrong? Why? Explain yourself!

t_ch: 脑子。
t_en: Brains.

t_ch: 你们得动动脑子。
t_en: Use your brains.

t_ch: 她好像坚决否认呢…要不，我们再想想？
t_en: She's pretty stubborn about denying it... Should we think it over again?

t_ch: 假设裁缝在说谎…
t_en: Suppose the tailor is lying...

t_ch: 假如姐姐说了谎，那么姐姐就是小偷。然后，厨子和小贩的证言就会相互矛盾……
t_en: If she's lying, then that makes her the thief! But then, what the cook and merchant said would contradict each other...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这到底是怎么回事啊？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what actually happened here!?

t_ch: …犯人可能另有其人。
t_en: ...The culprit might be somebody else.

t_ch: 喂！你到底靠不靠谱啊？
t_en: What! Can I even count on you?

t_ch: 算了算了，我们再去找找犯人的线索吧！
t_en: Forget it, let's keep looking for the culprit!

t_ch: 确实是我弄错了…
t_en: I had it wrong...

t_ch: 喂！你到底靠不靠谱啊？
t_en: What! Can I even count on you?

t_ch: 算了算了，我们再去找找犯人的线索吧！
t_en: Forget it, let's keep looking for the culprit!

t_ch: 虎克知道了，偷走老爹探测器的犯人就是你！
t_en: Hook figured it out, you're the one who stole daddy's detector!

t_ch: 有没有搞错啊…你们推理了半天，结果就这？
t_en: You gotta be kidding me... You thought about it for that long, and came up with this conclusion?

t_ch: 啊？你的意思是…我们搞错了？
t_en: Ah? You mean... we got it wrong?

t_ch: 叔叔我啊，都把真相告诉你们了！就这水平还想做侦探？还是回去过家家吧！
t_en: I gave you the answer, and you still couldn't crack the case, detective!? Why don't you just go home!

t_ch: 话不用说得这么绝吧。
t_en: You don't have to be so mean.

t_ch: 叔叔我呀，都告诉你了，小偷就是厨子！就算你问我一百遍，小偷就是厨子！
t_en: I already told you, it was the cook! Even if you asked me a hundred times, my answer would still be the cook!

t_ch: 你只是在嘴硬吧？
t_en: You're just trying to trick us.

t_ch: 哈？！你这么不愿意承认自己搞错了吗？你懂不懂什么叫推理啊！
t_en: Huh!? Is it that hard for you to admit you were wrong? Have you ever heard of something called "logic"?

t_ch: 你等着，虎克接下来就去找到证据，到时候你就会向虎克求饶了！
t_en: Just you wait. I'll find evidence, and then you'll be begging me for mercy!

t_ch: 行啊，那你们快去找吧！
t_en: Fine, go ahead and look!

t_ch: 难道真的不是你？
t_en: Is it really not you?

t_ch: 当然不是叔叔我了！
t_en: Of course it's not me!

t_ch: 喂，你怎么动摇了？我们再去找找证据，让他向虎克求饶吧！
t_en: Hey, you're not questioning yourself, are you? Let's look for evidence and let him beg me for mercy!

t_ch: 还向你求饶，不可能，你去找吧，你找到就明白了。
t_en: Pff, right, I'd beg you for mercy. Go ahead, you'll understand once you find the truth.

t_ch: 我确实弄错了，对不起。
t_en: I was wrong, my apologies.

t_ch: 我才不会骗你们，小偷就是厨子，就是厨子，就是厨子！
t_en: I wouldn't lie to you, it was the cook. The cook did it, the cook!

t_ch: 呣呣呣…我们还是再去找找吧，看看还有没有其他线索。
t_en: Hmm... Let's go look around and see if there are any more clues.

t_ch: 虎克知道了，偷走老爹探测器的犯人就是你！
t_en: Hook figured it out, you're the one who stole daddy's detector!

t_ch: 冤枉啊，我没偷东西啊！呜呜呜…早知道会被你们怀疑，我就不该在那个时候回到避难所；我要是不回到避难所……
t_en: Wha— What? You have the wrong person, I didn't steal anything! Waaaah... I knew you'd suspect me. I shouldn't have returned to the shelter at that time. If only I hadn't returned to the shelter...

t_ch: 要哭就到「地火」那去哭吧。
t_en: If you want to cry, go cry to Wildfire.

t_ch: 真的不是我干的，我什么都不知道啊！
t_en: I really didn't do it, I don't know anything!

t_ch: 你们…你们是不是觉得我好欺负，所以才这么对我！呜……
t_en: You... you just think I'm a pushover. That's why you're treating me like this! Wah...

t_ch: 你从帐篷里拿走了什么？
t_en: What did you take from the tent?

t_ch: 帐、帐篷？我从来没接近过费斯曼老爷子的帐篷啊……
t_en: T—Tent? I've never even been close to Mr. Fersman's tent...

t_ch: 无关个人，这是推理的结果。
t_en: This isn't personal, it's just deductive reasoning.

t_ch: 推理…结果错了，算什么推理嘛！
t_en: W— Well... you deduced wrong! What kind of reasoning are you even using!?

t_ch: 求求你们了，我这辈子连爸妈的零花钱都没偷过，可不要冤枉好人啊……
t_en: I beg you, I never even stole pocket money from my parents as a kid. Don't wrong an innocent person...

t_ch: 呣…也许小偷真的不是他，虎克觉得不像……
t_en: Hmm... Maybe he really isn't the thief. Hook doesn't think he's lying...

t_ch: 你在质疑我的推理？
t_en: Are you questioning my reasoning?

t_ch: 我的推理好像有点儿问题…
t_en: My deduction might be a little flawed...

t_ch: 你想呀！斯薇塔姐姐说了，应该只有真正的小偷会对我们说谎。
t_en: Just think about it! Swetta said that only the real thief would lie to us.

t_ch: 如果犯人真的是保姆哥哥的话，那他和小贩两个人的证言就都是假的了！
t_en: If the thief really is the helper, then what he and the merchant said would both be wrong!

t_ch: 是我错了……
t_en: My mistake...

t_ch: 之前怎么没见你这么机灵？
t_en: Why haven't I noticed you being this smart before?

t_ch: 可恶，大人真不靠谱！走，我们去找找别的线索！
t_en: Hmph! Adults are all so unreliable! Let's go look for more clues.

t_ch: 我们去老爹存放探测器的地方找找，说不定还能找到些线索。
t_en: Let's go look around where daddy kept the detector. Maybe we can find some clues there.

t_ch: 这是费斯曼在避难所里临时借用的帐篷，看得出有翻动过的痕迹，地上似乎掉落了一个<color=#dbc291ff>货箱标签</color>。
t_en: This is the tent that Fersman temporarily borrowed in the shelter. It looks like it has been rummaged and a <color=#dbc291ff>packaging label</color> has fallen on the ground.

t_ch: 这是物资箱子上的标签…虎克知道那些箱子堆在哪里！
t_en: This is the label from a supply box... I know where those boxes are!

t_ch: 就在那个方向，我们赶紧去看看吧！
t_en: That way, let's go take a look!

t_ch: 费斯曼使用的十字镐。原本应该是退休的年纪，但是为了生计，费斯曼还是会和流浪者一起到矿道里做工。
t_en: The pickaxe used by Fersman. He's supposed to be retired, but to make a living, Fersaman would still work in the mines with the vagrants.

t_ch: 避难所中的物资品箱子，经过长途跋涉搬运到这里。通过箱子上的印刷文字勉强可以认出是：铆钉镇。
t_en: The supply crate from the shelter, transported here after a long journey. Barely recognizable, some text is printed on the box: Rivet Town.

t_ch: 避难所中的物资品箱子，空空如也。从标签上来看，应该是用来存储应急帐篷的货箱。
t_en: An empty supply crate from the shelter. Judging by its label, it was used to store emergency tents.

t_ch: 避难所中的物资品箱子，里面湿漉漉的，就像是从地下河水里打捞起来的一样。从标签上来看，应该是用来存储应急食品的货箱。
t_en: A supply crate from the shelter. It's wet, as if it has been salvaged from the underground river. Judging by its label, it was used to store emergency food.

t_ch: 桑博叔叔总是去诊所找娜塔莎姐姐，虎克还以为他是生了什么大病呢！
t_en: Mr. Sampo's always at the clinic hanging around Miss Natasha. Hook thought he was really sick or something!

t_ch: 如果他和小偷是一帮的…那他也是坏家伙！
t_en: If he's working with the thief... then he's also a bad guy!

t_ch: 哥们儿，你当真？这玩意可不多见啊，你真愿意这个价格卖我？
t_en: My friend, are you serious? This device is quite rare. Are you really willing to sell it to me at such a low price?

t_ch: 我桑博虽说惜财如命，但这点判断力可还是有的——这里面不会有诈吧？
t_en: I mean, I love a sweet deal as much as anyone, but I still have common sense — there has to be a catch, right?

t_ch: 不是，你这家伙，废话怎么这么多？要还是不要，你赶紧给个准数！
t_en: No, no, why are you making this so complicated? Do you want it or not? Just tell me!

t_ch: 你们两个，都别想逃！漆黑的虎克大人到了！
t_en: You two, stop right there! Pitch-Dark Hook the Great has arrived!

t_ch: 桑博，你在这儿干嘛？
t_en: Sampo, what are you doing here?

t_ch: 不就是做点小生意嘛，干嘛这么大惊小怪的？
t_en: Just doing a little business. What's all the fuss?

t_ch: 那家伙是小偷，他想销赃！
t_en: That guy is a thief, he's trying to sell something he stole!

t_ch: 哎哟，便宜没好货，我就知道你这玩意来头有问题！
t_en: Tsk boy, I knew it was too good to be true!

t_ch: 那家伙是小偷，他想销赃！
t_en: That guy is a thief, he's trying to sell something he stole!

t_ch: 哎哟，便宜没好货，我就知道你这玩意来头有问题！
t_en: Tsk boy, I knew it was too good to be true!

t_ch: 事先说好，我和这货可不是一伙的啊，你别误会——虎克小妹妹，你别拿那玩意指着我啊，怪吓人的！
t_en: I gotta make this clear: I have nothing to do with this fella! You have to trust me— Miss Hook, please don't point that thing at me, it's scary!

t_ch: 喂，你这家伙，翻脸怎么比翻书还快？！说好的长期生意——
t_en: Hey! How can you sell me out like that! We had a deal...

t_ch: 你——就是你把老爹的宝贝偷走的，洞洞机绝对不会饶了你！
t_en: You! You stole daddy's treasure! Diggertron will not spare you...!

t_ch: 这…这都要怪费斯曼那个老头！就凭他有我手里的这玩意，就一个人发横财…这根本不公平！
t_en: This... this is all that old Fersman's fault! Just because he has this device I'm holding, he can make a fortune... It's not fair!

t_ch: 呃，那个…气氛好像有点紧张啊，要不我先撤了？
t_en: Umm, well... It seems you guys have some issues to sort out. So... maybe I should leave.

t_ch: 这不是你偷窃的理由。
t_en: That's no reason for you to steal it.

t_ch: 你跟我讲什么大道理？下层都到这个地步了，不靠偷不靠抢，我们这群人还能混得下去吗？
t_en: What kind of a reason are you looking for? With the Underworld in such a sad state, how are we supposed to survive if we don't resort to robbing and stealing?

t_ch: 费斯曼有充分的原因…
t_en: Fersman has his reasons...

t_ch: 我管他有什么苦衷！下层都到这个地步了，不靠偷不靠抢，我们这群人还能混得下去吗？
t_en: The heck if I care! With the Underworld in such a sad state, how are we supposed to survive if we don't resort to robbing and stealing?

t_ch: 哼，反正现在宝物在我手上——你们有种就抢回去啊！
t_en: Hmph, anyway, I got the device now! Come take it... if you have the guts!

t_ch: 得给你点苦头尝尝。
t_en: Guess you want to do this the hard way.

t_ch: 好啊，我们这就抢回去！
t_en: Okay, we'll take it then!

t_ch: 洞洞机，要准备上咯！
t_en: Diggertron... get ready!

t_ch: 你们…别以为我会怕你们！
t_en: ...I'm not afraid of you!

t_ch: 住口！先让我打你一顿！
t_en: Quiet! I'm going to give you a good butt-kicking!

t_ch: 等会儿！有话好说！是不是这货的来路有问题？跟我无关啊——
t_en: Wait a second! Let's all just calm down! Is there something wrong with the source of this stuff? I mean, I've got nothing to do with it...

t_ch: 居然有人敢挑衅我们…
t_en: You got some guts taunting us yourself...

t_ch: 哈，刚才还很嚣张，结果就这点实力嘛！快，把探测器拿过来给虎克看看！
t_en: Hah, you sure talked tough for someone who can't fight! Come on, hand the detector over to Hook!

t_ch: 糟…糟了！老爹的宝贝…坏了！
t_en: No... No! Daddy's treasure... It's broken!

t_ch: 人生总是伴着意外，要习惯…
t_en: Life is full of disappointments, sorry kid...

t_ch: 可是…可是……
t_en: But... But...

t_ch: 还能修好吗？
t_en: Can it be repaired?

t_ch: 嘿，这时候就要轮到桑博登场了啊！
t_en: Hey, now this is Sampo's time to shine!

t_ch: 你还在呢？
t_en: You're still here?

t_ch: 当然在！你和虎克小妹妹战斗时的英姿，我可是一秒都不想错过啊，嘿。
t_en: Of course! I wouldn't want to miss out on seeing you and Miss Hook's acts of heroism, would I?

t_ch: 告诉你们吧，磐岩镇有个叫「矿灯」巴列维的家伙，是个鉴定商。他表面自称不再鉴定古代遗物了，但其实背地里还在做高价回收的生意。
t_en: Listen here, there's a fellow in Boulder Town they call Balaway the Miner's Lamp. He's an appraiser. Although he claims that he doesn't do appraisals anymore for ancient relics, he actually still does a bit behind the scenes for ones that are of especially high value.

t_ch: 我看你们这件宝贝价值不菲，不如去敲打敲打他，碰碰运气。
t_en: I think this device of yours is probably worth a lot. It might be a good idea to try your luck with Balaway.

t_ch: 教你们一招——先在他面前装作要低价脱手的样子，等他兴冲冲地帮你修好了宝贝，再告诉他你们反悔了，想自己留着。
t_en: Here's a trick — first, pretend that you want to sell it for a low price, then wait for him to excitedly fix the device for you. After it's fixed, tell him you've changed your mind and you want to keep it for yourself.

t_ch: 这种商业模式就叫「小便宜一次占个够」，怎么样？
t_en: This business tactic is what I call a "bait and switch." Pretty clever, huh?

t_ch: 「矿灯」巴列维…我知道了！谢谢你，桑博叔叔。
t_en: Balaway the Miner's Lamp... Got it! Thanks, Mr. Sampo.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们这就回镇上找他吧！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's go into town to find him!

t_ch: 好走不送！
t_en: Have a safe trip!

t_ch: 想让你看看这个探测器。
t_en: I was wondering if you could have a look at this detector.

t_ch: 我早就不鉴定古代遗物了，除非这探测器是什么稀罕的宝贝……
t_en: I'm already retired from appraising ancient relics. So unless this detector is some kind of precious treasure...

t_ch: 就是这个！探针断掉了，还能修好吗？
t_en: Look at this! The detector's probe broke off, can you fix it?

t_ch: 哦哟哟，这还真是个稀罕货！让我仔细看看……
t_en: Oh ho ho, this certainly is a rare relic! Let me take a closer look...

t_ch: 仔细看看！一定要帮虎克修好它！
t_en: Yes, look extra close! You have to help Hook fix it!

t_ch: 这东西…我也算从业多年了，但还从来没见过这样的古代遗物。
t_en: This device... I've been in the business for a long time, but never have I seen an ancient relic like this one before.

t_ch: 依我看，这玩意多半是修不好了。不过毕竟是个难得一见的宝贝，就算坏了，我还是可以出高价回收。怎么样，你们考虑一下？
t_en: I think this is probably beyond repair. But still, it is a rare treasure. Even broken, I'd be willing to pay a high price for it. What do you say?

t_ch: 你这鉴定靠谱吗？
t_en: Can we trust your appraisal?

t_ch: 你再多讲讲，我是打算出手的。
t_en: Can you tell me more about it? I intend to sell it.

t_ch: 这探测器的探针都没了，就像是没了眼睛耳朵，现在就是堆破烂，一点实用价值都没有。
t_en: The detector's probe is gone, it's like searching for something without eyes or ears. Now it's nothing more than a pile of junk with no practical value.

t_ch: 我愿意给这玩意开价，已经算是对你们仁至义尽了。到底卖不卖，你给我个准！
t_en: I'm willing to offer a generous price for this thing. So, do you want to sell it or not?

t_ch: 虎克老大，我们回去吧。
t_en: Boss, I think we should go.

t_ch: 要不还是让费斯曼决定吧。
t_en: Maybe we should let Fersman decide what to do with it.

t_ch: 好吧，我们回去找老爹……
t_en: Alright then, let's go find daddy...

t_ch: 这探测器的探针都没了，就像是没了眼睛耳朵，根本没有人会买这些破烂。
t_en: The detector's probe is gone, it's like searching for something without eyes or ears. Who's going to want a pile of junk?

t_ch: 过了我这村儿，你也没有别的店儿能卖了。别犹豫了，我给你开个好价格。
t_en: I don't believe any other stores will buy it off you. Come on, I'll give you a nice deal.

t_ch: 奸商！我们是不会卖的。
t_en: We aren't going to sell it to you, you crooked profiteer!

t_ch: 你跑哪去了？我还正打算找你呢，虎克。走吧，回家，老爹给你做盐焗岩居蟹。
t_en: Where have you been, Hook? I was just about to go looking for you. Come on, let's go home, I'll cook some salt-baked rock crabs for you.

t_ch: 怎么了，这孩子？
t_en: What's wrong with this kid?

t_ch: 从刚才开始就一直这样…
t_en: She's been like this ever since just now...

t_ch: 你们刚才干什么去了？
t_en: Why, what happened?

t_ch: 我们找到了矿脉探测器。
t_en: We found the vein detector.

t_ch: 探测器？哦…是我之前弄丢的那件吗？
t_en: Vein detector? The one I lost?

t_ch: 虎克和{NICKNAME}帮老爹抓到了偷东西的小偷！
t_en: {NICKNAME} and Hook helped daddy catch the thief that stole it!

t_ch: 但是老爹的探测器…已经坏掉了。都怪虎克没有早点找到，不然……
t_en: But... daddy's detector... is broken. It's all my fault... If only I could've found it sooner...

t_ch: 果然是被人偷走了啊…你没有受伤吧，虎克？
t_en: So it was stolen... You're not hurt are you, Hook?

t_ch: 虎克没事！老爹，你快看看这探测器还能用吗？
t_en: I'm fine! Daddy, can you see if your detector still works?

t_ch: 唔…地髓探针已经坏掉了，这东西怕是已经运转不了了。
t_en: Hmm... the Geomarrow Probe is damaged. I'm afraid this thing won't work anymore.

t_ch: 对不起，老爹，虎克什么也没做到……
t_en: I'm sorry, daddy. I didn't help one bit...

t_ch: 为啥要跟我道歉？谢谢你为老爹费这么多心，虎克。
t_en: What are you apologizing for? Thank you so much for looking out for me, Hook.

t_ch: 可是，老爹的宝贝…呜呜——
t_en: But, daddy's treasure... Waaaah...

t_ch: 别哭，虎克！老爹的宝贝一直都在，从来都没丢过，更没有坏过。
t_en: Don't cry, Hook! Daddy's true treasure has been here all along. It was never lost or broken.

t_ch: 诶？
t_en: ...Huh?

t_ch: 的确是一件很可爱的宝贝。
t_en: It is an adorable treasure indeed.

t_ch: 啊？连你都知道是什么吗！
t_en: Huh, even you know what it is?

t_ch: 这宝贝还会哭呢。
t_en: This treasure can even cry, you know?

t_ch: 会…会哭？
t_en: It can... cry?

t_ch: 老爹靠着探测器找到了很多矿脉，也帮矿队省掉了不少开支。从这个角度看，它确实称得上是个好东西。
t_en: The detector helped me find lots of Geomarrow veins, and it saved the mining team a lot of money. In that respect, it was a very good thing.

t_ch: 但归根到底，探测器也就是一件工具而已。真正的宝贝…对于拥有它的人来说，一定有着不可替代的意义。
t_en: But at the end of the day, the detector is just a tool. A true treasure must have irreplaceable importance to someone.

t_ch: 虎克…没太听明白。
t_en: Hook... doesn't really understand.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，算了算了，都怪老爹说了些奇怪的话。总有一天你会明白的。
t_en: Hahaha, that's okay. I'm saying some things that might seem strange, but you will understand some day.

t_ch: 那老爹说的宝贝……
t_en: So, the treasure daddy is talking about...

t_ch: 完完整整，还活蹦乱跳的，你放心好了。
t_en: Is still happily running and jumping around.

t_ch: 老爹——
t_en: Daddy...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不准看虎克哭的样子，快出去！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're not allowed to see Hook cry! Go away!

t_ch: 哎哟，是{NICKNAME}啊，总算把你给盼来了！快快快，帮我个忙，咱俩关系这么好，你不会见死不救吧?
t_en: Oh, it's {NICKNAME}. You are finally here! Come on, do me a favor. We are such good friends. You won't just watch me suffer, right?

t_ch: 我跟你不熟…
t_en: We are not that close...

t_ch: 哎哟，你这话就生分了啊。咱俩怎么说也是一起出生入死的交情，转眼就不认人了，这么薄情？
t_en: Oh, that's a bit harsh! We've been through ups and downs together, and now you just pretend you don't know me. How can you be so cruel?

t_ch: 有事才找我，把我当工具人？
t_en: You only come to me when you need help. You think I'm a robot?

t_ch: 这话我可不爱听啊——我桑博是那种人吗？生意伙伴间讲究的就是一个相互信任，我怎么会把你当工具人呢？冤枉，天大的冤枉！
t_en: Come on! Am I like that to you? Businessmen like us rely on nothing but trust. Why would I treat you like a robot? No! Of course I wouldn't!

t_ch: {M#老弟}{F#老妹}，你就帮个忙呗——我在矿区搞了个矿具租赁的生意，但是有几件关键的矿具丢了，想让你帮忙找回来——多简单一事，对你来说还不是小菜一碟？
t_en: Just do me a favor — I started a mining tool rental business in the mines, but I'm missing a few key tools. I want you to help me find them. How simple is that? A piece of cake, right?

t_ch: 这矿具租赁的投入成本还是挺大的，想找到供货渠道可真不容易。我自己亏本倒没什么，关键有些人还指望着靠它挣钱养家呢。
t_en: The investment cost of this mining tool rental is still very high, and it's not easy to find a supplier. But I'm not worried about losing money. What matters is that some people really rely on this business to earn money to support their families.

t_ch: 到底发生了什么？
t_en: What happened here?

t_ch: 你还在乎别人养不养家呢？
t_en: Do you really care whether other people can support their families?

t_ch: 这叫什么话，我桑博向来深明大义——想想我过去帮过你的忙，这点你总不能否认吧？
t_en: What do you mean by that? I've always had a big heart — think about how helpful I've been to you in the past. You can't deny it, can you?

t_ch: 咳，简单来说，其实就是矿区之前发生了些小意外，你懂的——那场面可是乱的很，我费了大价钱买来的矿具全被搞丢了，把我气得够呛！
t_en: Well, to put it simply, there were some minor accidents in the mining area earlier. You know what it's like — it was chaos. All the mining tools I bought at a high price were lost, and I was furious!

t_ch: 恰好之前又有客户预约了我那套装备…按理说我本该自己去找的，但这矿区形势错综复杂，有些地方听说还有裂界怪物出没……
t_en: Coincidentally, not long ago, a customer ordered a set of equipment from me... Well... I'd like to go look for it myself, but the situation in the mining area is complicated. I hear there are Fragmentum monsters in some places...

t_ch: 哎，我们一般人压根靠近不了。我想着你身手不凡、友遍天下，才想找你帮个忙跑一趟，肯定比我这四处抓瞎的强！
t_en: Gosh, ordinary people like us can't get close at all. I want to ask you for a favor because you have such extraordinary skills and great connections. You are much better than me at this sort of thing!

t_ch: 我想想，一共丢了三件矿具…找起来是麻烦了点，不过你要是能帮我找回三件装备，我就给你一张绝密的藏宝图——怎么样，是不是很诱人啊？
t_en: Let me see, I lost three mining tools... It's a little hard to find them, but if you can help me find those three pieces of equipment, I'll give you a secret treasure map — how about that? A very attractive deal for you, wouldn't you say?

t_ch: 三件太多了，一件吧。
t_en: Three is too many. Can we make it one?

t_ch: 这怎么行！你是不知道在这地底，想整一套合规的矿具有多麻烦，哎，我这回可是破了不少财啊……
t_en: Come on! You don't know how much trouble one has to go through just to get a set of legit mining tools here underground. Heck! I've already invested so much money in this...

t_ch: 藏宝图？我听着像诈骗…
t_en: Treasure map? That sounds like a fraud...

t_ch: 这藏宝图绝对保真！老桑博做生意向来以诚信为本，你可千万别被那些嫉妒我生财有道的人给洗脑了啊！
t_en: This treasure map is absolutely real! I always do business with the utmost integrity. Don't be fooled by those scoundrels who are just jealous of me simply because I make good money!

t_ch: 矿队规定，矿工必须凑齐三件装备才能下矿——我这要不把三件套一次性集齐咯，就根本租不出去。{M#好兄弟}{F#好妹妹}，你就体谅一下哥哥的难处吧！
t_en: The mining team has a rule that all miners must have all three pieces of equipment before they can go down the mine — I can't rent them out if I don't have all three of them. {M#My good man}{F#My dear lady}, please have some sympathy for me!

t_ch: 三件实在太多了，我没时间。
t_en: Three is too many. I don't have time for that.

t_ch: 只拿回一件我也没法开工啊…咱们再商量商量？跟我合作肯定不吃亏……
t_en: I can't get to work with just one... Why don't we discuss this further? I can promise you won't regret working with me...

t_ch: 只找一件，不行就拉倒。
t_en: Just one. Otherwise, forget it.

t_ch: 别啊，这地底很难找到合适的人了，哎…等会儿，我得想想……
t_en: Please don't! It's hard to find the right person for the job around here. Jeez... Wait a minute...

t_ch: 要不这样吧，一口价，你帮我找回两件矿具，然后我就给你藏宝图，怎么样？
t_en: How about this? Help me find two pieces of mining equipment, and I'll give you the treasure map. How's that sound to you?

t_ch: 至于缺的矿具…哎，只能再加点钱找调休的矿工借了。也不知道现在谁在放假……
t_en: As for the missing third tool... Heck... I suppose I'll have to fork out a little extra money to rent it from the miners on leave. I don't even know who is on holiday right now...

t_ch: …行吧。
t_en: ...Fine.

t_ch: 我就知道，咱俩谁跟谁啊，你肯定会帮我的！
t_en: I knew it! We are such close mates. I knew you'd help a brother out!

t_ch: 好了，我也不废话了，等我看看清单…一件是<color=#dbc291ff>热驱动机械矿镐</color>，供货的说他是在下班归途中丢的，估计就在这附近，这个简单。
t_en: Well, enough chit-chat. Let me see the list... There is one called the <color=#dbc291ff>Thermal-Powered Mechanical Mining Pick</color>. The supplier said he lost it on his way home from work. Guess it's somewhere around here. This one should be simple.

t_ch: 另一件是<color=#dbc291ff>定制粉色安全帽</color>，稍微麻烦点…好像是被几个流浪者抢走了，得请你想办法要回来。
t_en: The other one is a <color=#dbc291ff>Customized Pink Safety Helmet</color>. This one is a little complicated... Looks like it got snatched by some vagrants. Might need you to find a way to get it back.

t_ch: 就这两件，跟说好的一样。那就劳烦您了，嘿嘿——合作愉快！
t_en: As we previously agreed, just these two! Thank you so much for your help. Hehe — It's a pleasure to work with you!

t_ch: 裸露的地表上立着些矿具，从表面的锈迹来看，似乎是矿工们多年来在下班路上积攒出的「成果」。
t_en: There are some mining tools left standing on the ground. The rust stains on the ground seem to be the result of miners dragging their tools on their way home over the years.

t_ch: 杂乱的矿具遮挡住了机械矿稿的存在，一不仔细看还难以分辨它的身形…这么看来，桑博先前的说辞应该是真的。
t_en: The mess of mining tools obscures the pick from view. It would be difficult to spot it at first glance... It seems what Sampo said earlier was true.

t_ch: 你谁啊，见到我也不打声招呼，懂不懂这里的规矩？
t_en: Who are you? Why didn't you give me a proper greeting? Don't you know the rules around here?

t_ch: 听说你很狂啊，抢人帽子？
t_en: I hear you're a tough guy, aren't you? Snatching other people's helmets?

t_ch: 什么抢人帽子？这是我在地上捡的。
t_en: What do you mean by snatching helmets? I picked it up off the ground.

t_ch: 地上还会长安全帽？
t_en: Do helmets grow from the ground now?

t_ch: 听不懂人话？都说了，是我捡的。下层区的规矩，谁捡到就归谁，劝你别多管闲事！
t_en: What? Don't you understand the human language? Like I said, I found it on the ground. That's the rule in the Underworld. Finders keepers. So, mind your own business!

t_ch: 别装了，这是桑博的帽子。
t_en: Quit messing around. This is Sampo's helmet.

t_ch: 桑什么…根本不认识，少来碰瓷！而且你搞清楚，这是我的地盘，劝你别多管闲事！
t_en: Sam... who? I don't even know who that is. Don't try to blackmail me! And this is my turf, so stay out of it!

t_ch: 啊，我明白了…你是故意找茬是吧？我看你是皮痒了！
t_en: Ah... I get it! You're here to pick a fight with me, aren't you?

t_ch: 来人啊，跟我一起教训教训这小崽子！
t_en: Hey! Come on, guys, let's teach this brat a lesson!

t_ch: （这么高调的颜色…等等，帽子上好像写了名字——「匹克」？）
t_en: (Such a high-profile color... Wait a minute, there seems to be a name written on the helmet — "Peak"?)

t_ch: （原来匹克就是桑博的供货商吗？算了，还是别多想了，回去找桑博领赏吧。）
t_en: (So, Peak is Sampo's supplier? Whatever, none of my business. I should head back to Sampo and collect the reward.)

t_ch: 你要的东西我拿回来了。
t_en: I got what you wanted.

t_ch: 嘿，瞧这效率！整个下层区，我打着地髓灯都找不到手脚比你还爽利的了！
t_en: Wow, so efficient! I sure can't find anyone better than you in this underworld, not even with the help of a Geomarrow lamp!

t_ch: 不过还是容我先验下货，看看是不是我要找的那几件……
t_en: Let me check the goods and make sure they're what I'm looking for...

t_ch: 说好的藏宝图呢？搞快点！
t_en: Where's my treasure map? Hurry up!

t_ch: 别着急，谈好的条件我肯定不会反悔，这就是走个形式而已。就算是大守护者跟我做生意，我也得象征性地走个流程嘛。
t_en: Relax... I never break my promises. This is just a routine inspection. I'd do the same even if I were dealing with the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 这么独一无二的颜色，还要验？
t_en: With a color that unique, do you even need to double check?

t_ch: 走个形式而已，你别这么严肃嘛。就算是大守护者跟我做生意，我也得象征性地走个流程嘛。
t_en: Come on, it's just procedure, don't take it so seriously! As I said, I'd do the same even if I were dealing with the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: …千真万确，这就是我要找的那两件矿具。给，说好的——这张藏宝图你收好，按着上面的标记找就行了，绝对有意外惊喜！
t_en: ...They're indeed the real deal! These are exactly the two mining tools I'm looking for. Here you go —Be sure to take good care of the treasure map. Just follow the mark on it and you sure will find yourself a surprise!

t_ch: 藏宝图上看上去像模像样，但细节之处却令人生疑。
t_en: This treasure map looks decent... But the details are questionable.

t_ch: 这图，保真吗…
t_en: Is this map... legit?

t_ch: 当然是真的！你不信的话去挖挖看不就知道了，我保证绝对能挖到好东西。
t_en: Of course, it is legit! Just go dig it up the treasure and see for yourself if you don't believe me. I promise there will be good stuff.

t_ch: 你这图是高仿的吧？
t_en: You sure this is not a fake?

t_ch: 这怎么可能，这图我是从一个老矿工手里捞到的，怎么会是高仿？
t_en: Nonsense! I fleeced this map from an old miner. No way it could be a fake!

t_ch: 哎哟，下层区的藏宝图都长这样儿的，绝对错不了，我怎么可能骗你呢？
t_en: Relax! All the treasure maps in the Underworld look just like this. No way I could be wrong. Just trust me on this!

t_ch: 行了行了，快去寻宝吧，我等你的好消息，嘿嘿……
t_en: Alright, alright, alright. Time for you to go treasure hunting. I'd be waiting for your good news, hehe...

t_ch: （这是什么宝贝…防毒面罩吗？等一会儿，这上面也有刻字…「匹克」？又是他？）
t_en: (What's this... a gas mask? Wait a minute, there are some characters engraved on it..."Peak"? This guy again?)

t_ch: （矿具…匹克…三件套…可恶，又被桑博耍了！）
t_en: (Mining tools... Peak... Set of three... Darn it! Sampo fooled me again!)

t_ch: （那个奸商…得回去找他算账才行。）
t_en: (That unscrupulous merchant... Now I have to go back and deal with him.)

t_ch: 急死我了…那三件矿具…你怎么还不动身啊！
t_en: I'm so anxious...Those three mining tools... Why don't you do something!

t_ch: 你放心好了，你的委托我已经派人加急帮你处理了，绝对不会耽搁的。
t_en: Don't worry, I've sent someone to handle this matter. Rest assured.

t_ch: 有人来了，这事我们晚点再谈。
t_en: Someone's coming. Let's talk about it later.

t_ch: 这么快就回来了？怎么样，那两件矿具找到了吗？
t_en: Back so soon? How's it going? Did you find those two mining tools?

t_ch: 哟，这就回来了——怎么样，挖到宝藏了吗？
t_en: Hey, how's it going? Did you find the treasure?

t_ch: 这就是你说的「宝藏」？
t_en: This is what you call "Treasure"?

t_ch: 居然又上了你的当…
t_en: Can't believe I fell for your tricks again...

t_ch: 嘿，东西你找到了？来来来，先给我，然后等我慢慢给你解释……
t_en: Hey, you found something? Come on, just hand it over and then I'll explain.

t_ch: 你想想，这矿具三件套，对矿工来说可不就是宝藏吗？这几件装备结合起来能产出的价值，比任何宝箱战利品都——
t_en: Think about it... aren't these three pieces of mining tools the real treasure to a miner? The total amount of value that could be produced by these pieces of equipment is surely more than any treasure that I know of—

t_ch: ——哎哟，别动手！你消消气，别这么激动，有话好好说——
t_en: —Hey, calm down! Take it easy. No need to get physical with me. We can just talk it over—

t_ch: 说啥啊，能先处理下我的问题吗？我明天可是有排班的，不带齐矿具我没法睡觉…啊不是，我没法上班啊！
t_en: What are you two talking about? Can you deal with my problem first? I have a shift tomorrow, I can't sleep without the mining equipment... Erh I mean... I can't work without the mining equipment!

t_ch: 别急，你看——我们{M#英俊潇洒}{F#美丽动人}的{NICKNAME}这不就给你送来了吗？
t_en: Relax! See— our {M#handsome}{F#beautiful} {NICKNAME} has brought this for you, hasn't {M#he}{F#she}?

t_ch: 还说不把我当「工具人」？
t_en: You are not treating me like a robot, huh?

t_ch: 我已经救你两次了，匹克…
t_en: I've saved you twice, Peak...

t_ch: 哦哦，原来是你，娜塔莎医生派来的外来者！太感谢了，没有你的话，我接下来这一个月怕是都睡不上安稳觉了……
t_en: Oh, it's you! The outsider sent by Dr. Natasha! Thank you so much. If not for you, I'm afraid I wouldn't be able to sleep for a whole month...

t_ch: …什么啊，原来你俩早就认识？嘁，早知道就不花那么大功夫忽悠——
t_en: ...What? You two already knew each other? Well...If I knew this in the first place, I wouldn't have spent so much effort trying to fool—

t_ch: ——啊不是，没啥，嘿嘿。匹克老弟，看见了吧，我桑博答应过的事哪有办不成的？得了，面罩收好，加上之前的两件，你的矿具就全齐了。
t_en: Oh, I mean, nothing! Hehe, you see, Peak, I always keep my promises! Alright, here's the gas mask, which means you've got all your mining tools now.

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这找东西的开销也得算到代理费里…看在咱俩的交情上，这次只多收你三成吧？
t_en: Hey, the cost of finding things has to be included in the agency fee... But since we are such good friends, I will only charge you <unbreak>30%</unbreak> more this time, okay?

t_ch: 三、三成？行…行吧。等会儿，我翻下有没有零钱……
t_en: <unbreak>30</unbreak>—<unbreak>30%</unbreak>? Alright... Wait, let me see if I've got any change...

t_ch: 所以这到底是怎么回事？
t_en: So what is going on?

t_ch: 别急嘛，我都说了，肯定会给你解释……
t_en: Relax... I'll explain everything to you...

t_ch: 你们俩合着伙来骗我？
t_en: So, you two worked together to scam me?

t_ch: 哎哟，别说这么晦气的话，等我慢慢给你解释……
t_en: Hey, don't say it like that. Just let me explain...

t_ch: 之前我和你说的矿具租赁生意，那都是确有其事。我打听了好久才知道，原来大矿区还有个一周只出工一天的匹克老弟——
t_en: The mining equipment rental business I told you about before is real. I've asked many people and I realized Peak worked only one day a week in the Great Mine.

t_ch: …总而言之，你不上工…啊不对，你「灵活出工」的时候，这身边的矿具闲着也是闲着，何不跟我合作赚一笔呢？
t_en: So anyway... When you are not working at a job... Um, or should I say, when you are in between jobs, your mining tools are just sitting there. So, the idea came to me. Why don't you just collaborate with me to rent out those tools so that we can make some good money together?

t_ch: 我跑来以后才发现，这哥们把自己那宝贝三件套全给弄丢了。我是商人，又不是拾荒的，这找东西的活可不对口啊…然后我这不就想到你了，嘿嘿。
t_en: It was only after I sorted all this out in my head that I learned that this guy had somehow managed to lose all three pieces of his precious equipment. You see, I'm a businessman, not a scavenger. I'm not at all interested in this sort of scavenger job. So, naturally, I immediately thought of you, hehe.

t_ch: 我可以揍你吗？
t_en: Can I punch you now?

t_ch: 我不管，给钱。
t_en: I don't care. Pay up.

t_ch: 消消气，消消气…你看，我这儿还有张货真价实的藏宝图，你收下吧。
t_en: Calm down, calm down! Look, I do have a genuine treasure map here for you.

t_ch: 我一直把它当压箱底的宝贝…我跟你说，这张藏宝图的前任主人，背后也有段曲折的经历——
t_en: I've been taking such good care of this precious of mine... You see, the former owner of this treasure map turned out to have quite the tragic story...

t_ch: 当初有个老矿头，出卖了自己所有兄弟才把它搞到手…可惜才拿到没多久，他某位兄弟的女儿上门复仇，图还没捂热，那家伙就「呜呼」了。
t_en: Once upon a time, there was this old miner who betrayed all his friends just to get his hands on this map. Unfortunately, not long after, one of his friends' daughters came for revenge and killed him.

t_ch: 如果你再骗我的话…
t_en: If you lie to me again...

t_ch: 可不敢，可不敢！再一再二不能再三，这点道理我可太懂了，嘿嘿。
t_en: I wouldn't dare no more! Fool you once, shame on you. Fool you twice... hehe... I won't do it again, I swear!

t_ch: 别废话了，赶紧拿来。
t_en: Stop jabbering! Hand it over!

t_ch: 只要你能消气，一切都好说，嘿嘿。
t_en: As long as you are not angry with me, then I will do whatever you say. Hehe!

t_ch: 这下好了，匹克老弟的矿具找回来了，我的租赁生意也能顺利进行，你也拿到了传奇藏宝图…这就叫「三赢」吧？
t_en: Well... Peak got his tools back, my renting business can continue and you got your treasure map... I bet we can say this is a win-win-win situation?

t_ch: 不介意的话，我现在想跟匹克老弟聊点商业机密…你还在等啥？赶紧去寻宝吧。
t_en: If you don't mind... I'd like to discuss some confidential business with Peak... What are you waiting for? Go find the treasure!

t_ch: （桑博这家伙…算了，再原谅他一次吧。）
t_en: (This guy... Ugh, forget it, just trust him one more time.)

t_ch: 咳咳，什么叫「只出工一天」？我那是灵活出工，做一天然后躺三天，这是保障我上班效率的独门准则。
t_en: Ahem... What do you mean by "only work for one day"? I am actually busy with my other business! Resting three days before starting another day of work can help me work more efficiently.

t_ch: 确实，这都被你发现了，桑博老哥的商业眼光真敏锐。
t_en: Indeed, you are right, Sampo. You really are the one with the business acumen.

t_ch: 我好像听过同事讲过类似的故事，没想到这藏宝图居然在你手里，真行啊。
t_en: I seem to have heard a colleague telling me a similar story. I'd never guess the treasure had fallen into your hands. You sure have your ways!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，过誉了，桑博我只是比较有眼光罢了。好了，这下你也该相信我了吧。
t_en: Hehe, you flatter me. I'm just gifted with the insight to do some good business, that's all. So, I think you can trust me now!

t_ch: …好像有哪里不对，但是听上去又很有道理…桑博老哥，你这脑子转得可真快。
t_en: Something seems to be off, but it sounds perfectly reasonable... Sampo, you truly are one smart fellow!

t_ch: 大矿区那边…会不会也受到刚才的战斗影响了啊？
t_en: ...Do you think the Great Mine was affected by our fighting on the surface?

t_ch: 但愿没有引起塌方事故。
t_en: Let's hope we didn't cause a collapse.

t_ch: 看，安东尼娜！那些矿工又和流浪者在一起。
t_en: Look, it's Antonia! The miners and vagrants are all in the same spot.

t_ch: 难道他们又要吵架了…快上去看看！
t_en: I sure hope they're not arguing again... Quick, let's head over!

t_ch: ——我们手上的物资也不富裕，这些你们就先拿去吧。
t_en: ...Here, take it. I'm afraid we're short on goods and materials right now.

t_ch: 这点玩意我们也不缺，你们留着……
t_en: Oh, well we actually have enough for the moment. You hang onto it...

t_ch: 喂！你们这帮家伙，是不是又在挑事？
t_en: Hey! Are you guys causing trouble again?

t_ch: 是你们？不不，大家都误会了——
t_en: It's you! Oh, no, no, you misunderstand...

t_ch: 矿区刚才经历了剧烈的震颤，又发生了小规模的塌方。多亏了这些路过的流浪者伸出援手，矿工弟兄们才得以全身而退。
t_en: There was a huge quake at the mine just now — part of it collapsed. If the vagrants here hadn't stopped to help, I doubt we'd have gotten everyone out in one piece.

t_ch: 我们刚刚就是在商量该怎么感谢他们，毕竟矿队现在也是囊中羞涩……
t_en: I was just discussing how to repay them. It's a shame the mining team is hard up right now.

t_ch: 告诉你们一个天大的好消息。
t_en: Wanna hear something really great?

t_ch: 好消息？和我们有关的？这我可得好好听听……
t_en: Oh? To do with us? I'm listening...

t_ch: 我来帮你们出钱。
t_en: I've got you covered.

t_ch: 呃…能让布洛妮娅帮咱们报销吗？
t_en: Uh... can't we get Bronya to cover the bill?

t_ch: 哎呀，跑偏了——{NICKNAME}，快把好消息告诉大家吧！
t_en: Ugh, can we focus? {NICKNAME}, tell them the good news already!

t_ch: 将胜利的消息分享给了矿队和流浪者们……
t_en: Telling the mining team and the vagrants about the victory...

t_ch: 你们的意思是…我们很快就能和上层区重新建立沟通了？
t_en: You mean to say that... we're about to be reunited with the Overworld?

t_ch: 简直难以置信！这…我这不会是在做梦吧？！这种好事真的会发生在我们身上吗？
t_en: Are you serious!? This has... gotta be a dream. Good things... don't happen to us!

t_ch: 才不是做梦！不信的话，你狠狠掐自己一把就知道啦。
t_en: Today they do! And you're not dreaming. If you don't believe us, pinch yourself!

t_ch: 如果一切都能恢复到十几年以前的样子…那矿队就不必生活在饥寒交迫之下了。
t_en: If things go back to the way they were <unbreak>10</unbreak> years ago... then the mining team can finally escape this poverty...

t_ch: …你们也不用再像现在这样寄人篱下，没有尊严地活着了。
t_en: ...and you won't have to rely on the charity of others. We'll finally be living with the dignity we deserve.
I... *sniff* *sob*

t_ch: 男儿有泪不轻弹…
t_en: Come on. Wipe your tears.

t_ch: 想哭就哭出来吧！
t_en: Let it out!

t_ch: 感谢你们，外来者！你们不止是矿队的救星，也是下层区所有人的英雄……
t_en: Thank you, outsiders, from the bottom of my heart! The mining team'll remember you as heroes... as will the rest of the Underworld.

t_ch: 麻烦你们转告娜塔莎，矿队会做好重新开工的准备——如果缆车站真的能开放，我们也会派矿工代表上去和筑城者交涉的。
t_en: Please tell Natasha that we're ready to get back to work... If the cable car station can start operating again, we'll dispatch a mining representative to negotiate with the Architects.

t_ch: …杨叔说得还挺客气的，总结起来就是——即使我们封印了星核，也没法一举改变这里的困境……
t_en: ...Mr. Yang put it nicely, but the reality is this: We sealed the Stellaron, but that's not gonna solve the problems on this planet...

t_ch: 突然有种不知道怎么面对大家的感觉。
t_en: There I was getting excited to tell everyone the good news...

t_ch: 毕竟我们承诺过…
t_en: We did make a promise...

t_ch: 我们尽力了，交给时间吧。
t_en: We did all we could. Now it's a matter of time...

t_ch: 原来我们只是画了个大饼？
t_en: Does this count as false advertising?

t_ch: 「星核」就是这般残酷。至少和我们曾去过的某些星球相比，这里还有希望。
t_en: That's the cruelty of a Stellaron. Still, compared with some of the other planets we've visited, at least this one has hope.

t_ch: 那…我们该把现在的状况告诉大家吗？
t_en: So... should we tell everyone the deal?

t_ch: 等时机合适，再转告布洛妮娅吧。现在，就别破坏这美好的氛围了。
t_en: We should tell Bronya when the time's right. Let's not dampen the spirit just yet.

t_ch: 向炮手各单位传令——坚守阵地，向步战单位前进方向提供徐进弹幕支援。
t_en: Put all artillery units on standby. Wait for my command to bombard enemy positions and cover our infantry advance.

t_ch: 此处地形狭窄，各步战单位结成双排线列阵型——近卫在前，射手在后。另切记，优先撤离伤员，严禁急功冒进。
t_en: We have a choke point here. Tell all infantry units to switch to double-line formation — close-quarters in front, gunners behind. Remember, get the wounded out as soon as possible, and always secure your flanks before advancing.

t_ch: 是！
t_en: Yes, sir!

t_ch: …啊，你们来了！准备好，接下来我们要配合步兵线列，向矿区纵深处开路。
t_en: Ah, you're here. Get ready, we need to help the infantry lines drive deep into the mine.

t_ch: 现在战况如何？
t_en: How's the situation?

t_ch: 矿区后方已经净空稳固，足够支援前线作战了…铁卫和「地火」组成的临时战斗序列正在缓慢推进、搜救伤员。
t_en: The back of the mine has been secured, which gives us a forward base to send in supplies. The Silvermane-Wildfire combined regiment is conducting rescue missions as they advance.

t_ch: 事态紧急，我必须亲临前线。指挥系统就暂时交给我的参谋接管。
t_en: The battle is fierce, I need to get to the front line. I'll leave the command post to my officers.

t_ch: 你也要亲自下场？
t_en: Do you have to go there in person?

t_ch: 事态紧急，必须如此。我会让信任的参谋暂时接管指挥系统。
t_en: The battle is reaching a pivotal point. I have to get to the front line. I'll leave the command post to my officers.

t_ch: 好在作战目标清晰，地形也不算复杂——我的部下经验丰富，不必担心指挥系统出差错。
t_en: We have clear objectives and the terrain is relatively easy to read. My officers are well-trained — the command system will be in good hands.

t_ch: 那个，我不怎么懂带兵…但是一军统帅怎么能轻易冲上前线？这次的状况和铁卫禁区那会儿可不一样啊……
t_en: I... I don't know much about warfare, but I don't see why a commander needs to lead the charge. The situation here is totally different from the Restricted Zone.

t_ch: 让我和{NICKNAME}去开路吧，老…杰帕德！你还是坐镇后方……
t_en: Gepard, let me and {NICKNAME} clear the way for you. You should stay in your command post.

t_ch: 谢谢你替我担心，姐姐。但请你相信我作为军官的判断。
t_en: Thanks, Serval, but please trust my judgment on this.

t_ch: 我已经向奥列格先生保证，要尽力挽救每一位居民的生命——我必须说到做到。
t_en: I made a promise to Mr. Oleg to save as many people as possible. I have to see to it myself.

t_ch: …想说动你可真是太难了。就听你的吧，老弟。
t_en: ...There's no stopping you once you've made up your mind. Fine, we'll do it your way.

t_ch: 我们出发吧，为铁卫和「地火」开路。
t_en: Let's move out. We need to clear a way for Wildfire and the Guards.

t_ch: 救命啊！
t_en: Help!

t_ch: 怪物、怪物来了！
t_en: Monsters!

t_ch: 我不想死…！
t_en: A—Agh! I don't wanna die...!

t_ch: 快逃啊！
t_en: R—Run for your lives!

t_ch: 救、救救我！
t_en: Help me!

t_ch: 非战斗人员请迅速向矿区大桥撤离！铁卫已经在建立防御阵线，可以保障你们的安全！
t_en: All civilians please go to the bridge immediately! The Silvermane Guards have set up a defensive perimeter — you'll be safe there!

t_ch: 裂界怪物冲着这边来了…怎么办，杰帕德？
t_en: There's a monster heading this way... What should we do, Gepard?

t_ch: 当然是打倒它——为大家断后。
t_en: Bring it down. We need to cover the retreat.

t_ch: 不堪一击…但不要放松警惕。
t_en: It made it easy for us... but we can't let our guards down.

t_ch: 这里安全了，继续前进吧。
t_en: It's safe here. Let's move on.

t_ch: 杰帕德，快看！前方似乎有矿民的临时阵地！
t_en: Gepard, look! The miners have put up a makeshift defensive line!

t_ch: 你…你们是银、银鬃铁卫吗？
t_en: A—Are you... a Silvermane Guard?

t_ch: 没错，我们正在和「地火」一起搜救被卷入战斗的矿民。除了你们，前方还有非战斗人员吗？
t_en: That's right. We're evacuating the miners together with Wildfire. Are there any other civilians up ahead?

t_ch: 铁卫…铁、铁卫和「地火」？你们居然一起……
t_en: Silvermane G—Guards and Wildfire are working together now...?

t_ch: 啊！前、前面…我知道，刚才有个女、女孩子…跑出去了！
t_en: Oh, Civilians... Ah! I saw a girl run out from our defensive position just now!

t_ch: 我兄弟也、也追出去了，可现在都没、没回来……
t_en: My brother went after her, but they haven't come back...

t_ch: 了解，我会把他们带回来的。铁卫和「地火」已经在大桥上布置了防御，你们先撤到那里去吧。
t_en: Understood, I'll bring them back. The Guards and Wildfire have set up defenses on the bridge. You should head over.
